document_id: SL 2017-57, Section 11C.6.(d) North Carolina Child Support Incentives

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated text in the CSS/theme wrapper that does not come from the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML head contains invented metadata/styling text such as "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", CSS variables like "--nc-font", "--nc-navy", and a Google Fonts import. None of this appears in the source PDF pages, which only contain the report content.

findings.description: The HTML nests a second <main> inside the first <main>, creating duplicated structural wrapper content not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML shows both "<main>" wrapping the document and another inner "<main>" around the body content. The PDF has a single linear document structure with no duplicated wrapper sections.

findings.description: A sentence in the ACTS modernization bullet list is mistranscribed, producing a grammatically broken and meaning-altered statement.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 bullet reads: "Completed the to-be business process analysis using a human-centered approach. This method will allow NCCSS to re-imagine the child support experience in order to uncover opportunities for process and policy improvements that guide technology decisions. Conduct the Completed to-be system analysis." HTML reproduces this as a single bullet and then also adds a separate bullet "Completed the to-be system analysis." The phrasing "Conduct the Completed to-be system analysis" is clearly erroneous and alters the text.
